text.title: Rails ActiveRecord Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Rails ActiveRecord methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Query Objects MUST accept a relation in `initialize(relation = Model.all)`, chain methods returning `self`, and expose a terminal `results` method returning the relation (never an array).
rule.rationale: Returning the relation defers execution and allows further scoping at the controller level; `to_a` freezes the query and forfeits composability.

rule.statement: Filters MUST live in named scopes (`scope :active, -> { where(deleted_at: nil) }`). `default_scope` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: `default_scope` is invisible at every call site; debugging "missing data" wastes an hour per incident.

rule.statement: Eager-loading MUST happen at the controller boundary via `.includes` / `.preload` / `.eager_load`. Views MUST NOT trigger relation accesses against un-loaded associations.
rule.rationale: View-triggered queries are invisible in code review; centralising at the controller makes query cost reviewable.

rule.statement: Loops over > 1000 records MUST use `find_each` or `in_batches`. `Model.all.each` on growable tables is forbidden.
rule.rationale: `all.each` loads the full result set; `find_each` streams in batches and keeps memory constant.

rule.statement: Where clauses MUST use hash conditions or `where(...)` with placeholders. String interpolation into `where("name = '#{input}'")` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: String-interpolated WHERE clauses are SQL-injection vectors; AR placeholders are safe by default.

rule.statement: CI MUST run a N+1 gate (Prosopite or Bullet in CI mode). Specs that trigger an N+1 MUST fail.
rule.rationale: Without an automated gate, agents reintroduce N+1 within one sprint of the last fix.
